packages.description: Macros 1.1 implementation of #[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)]
packages.name: serde_derive

packages.description:
Conditional move CPU intrinsics which are guaranteed on major platforms (ARM32/ARM64, x86/x86_64,
RISC-V) to execute in constant-time and not be rewritten as branches by the compiler. Provides
wrappers for the CMOV family of instructions on x86/x86_64 and CSEL on AArch64, along with a
portable "best-effort" pure Rust fallback implementation.
packages.name: cmov

packages.description: Fork-aware no_std Ethereum protocol validation state.
packages.name: eth-valkyoth-protocol

packages.description: Determine whether characters have the XID_Start or XID_Continue properties according to Unicode Standard Annex #31
packages.name: unicode-ident

packages.description:
Pure Rust implementation of the Elliptic Curve Digital Signature Algorithm (ECDSA) as specified in
FIPS 186-4 (Digital Signature Standard), implemented generically over elliptic curves, providing
RFC6979 deterministic signing support as well as randomized signatures and signature verification
packages.name: ecdsa

packages.description: Future Reth integration boundary for eth.
packages.name: eth-valkyoth-reth

packages.description:
Securely clear secrets from memory with a simple trait built on
stable Rust primitives which guarantee memory is zeroed using an
operation will not be 'optimized away' by the compiler.
Uses a portable pure Rust implementation that works everywhere,
even WASM!
packages.name: zeroize

packages.description: A generic serialization/deserialization framework
packages.name: serde

packages.description: A JSON serialization file format
packages.name: serde_json

packages.description: A substitute implementation of the compiler's `proc_macro` API to decouple token-based libraries from the procedural macro use case.
packages.name: proc-macro2

packages.description: Core random number generation traits and tools for implementation.
packages.name: rand_core

packages.description:
Pure Rust implementation of RFC6979: Deterministic Usage of the Digital Signature Algorithm (DSA)
and Elliptic Curve Digital Signature Algorithm (ECDSA)
packages.name: rfc6979

packages.description:
Pure Rust implementation of the SHA-2 hash function family
including SHA-224, SHA-256, SHA-384, and SHA-512.
packages.name: sha2

packages.description: Serde traits only, with no support for derive -- use the `serde` crate instead
packages.name: serde_core

packages.description: no_std Ethereum Keccak-256 hashing trait boundary.
packages.name: eth-valkyoth-hash

packages.description: Numeric traits for generic mathematics
packages.name: num-traits

packages.description:
wNAF (w-ary non-adjacent form) variable-time scalar multiplication implemented generically
over elliptic curve groups with full `no_alloc` support, including multiscalar multiplication using
Straus's interleaved window method. Contains an implementation adapted from the `group` crate which
has been forked and enhanced but is otherwise compatible with that crate's traits, along with the
`ff` crate's traits for finite field representations
packages.name: wnaf

packages.description: Dependency-free no_std secret memory sanitization with safe defaults and an explicit volatile wipe backend.
packages.name: sanitization

packages.description: Future signer isolation boundary for eth.
packages.name: eth-valkyoth-signer

packages.description: Pure-Rust traits and utilities for constant-time cryptographic implementations.
packages.name: subtle

packages.description: Raw FFI bindings to platform libraries like libc.
packages.name: libc

packages.description: Elliptic curve group traits and utilities
packages.name: group

packages.description:
Pure Rust implementation of complete addition formulas for prime order elliptic
curves (Renes-Costello-Batina 2015). Generic over field elements and curve
equation coefficients
packages.name: primeorder

packages.description: Fast integer primitive to string conversion
packages.name: itoa

packages.description: Traits for cryptographic hash functions and message authentication codes
packages.name: digest

packages.description:
Lightweight runtime CPU feature detection for aarch64, loongarch64, and x86/x86_64 targets,
with no_std support and support for mobile targets including Android and iOS
packages.name: cpufeatures

packages.description: A double-to-string conversion algorithm based on Schubfach and yy
packages.name: zmij

packages.description: Dependency-free no_std Ethereum EVM core domains for eth.
packages.name: eth-valkyoth-evm-core

packages.description:
secp256k1 elliptic curve library written in pure Rust with support for ECDSA
signing/verification/public-key recovery, Taproot Schnorr signatures (BIP340),
Elliptic Curve Diffie-Hellman (ECDH), and general-purpose secp256k1 elliptic
curve group operations which can be used to implement arbitrary protocols
packages.name: k256

packages.description: Buffer types for block processing of data
packages.name: block-buffer

packages.description: Core no_std Ethereum protocol primitives.
packages.name: eth-valkyoth-primitives

packages.description: Traits for cryptographic signature algorithms (e.g. ECDSA, Ed25519)
packages.name: signature

packages.description:
Const-friendly implementation of the ISO/IEC Object Identifier (OID) standard
as defined in ITU X.660, with support for BER/DER encoding/decoding as well as
heapless no_std (i.e. embedded) support
packages.name: const-oid

packages.description:
Pure Rust implementation of a big integer library which has been designed from
the ground-up for use in cryptographic applications. Provides constant-time,
no_std-friendly implementations of modern formulas using const generics.
packages.name: crypto-bigint

packages.description:
Pure Rust embedded-friendly implementation of the Distinguished Encoding Rules
(DER) for Abstract Syntax Notation One (ASN.1) as described in ITU X.690 with
full support for heapless `no_std`/`no_alloc` targets
packages.name: der

packages.description: Library for building and interfacing with finite fields
packages.name: ff

packages.description: Bounded no_std Ethereum wire codec policy.
packages.name: eth-valkyoth-codec

packages.description:
A macro to ergonomically define an item depending on a large number of #[cfg]
parameters. Structured like an if-else chain, the first matching branch is the
item that gets emitted.
packages.name: cfg-if

packages.description: Common traits used by cryptographic algorithms
packages.name: crypto-common

packages.description: Security-first no_std facade for the evolving full Ethereum stack.
packages.name: eth

packages.description: Future RPC trust-policy boundary for eth.
packages.name: eth-valkyoth-rpc

packages.description: An implementation of Keccak derived functions.
packages.name: tiny-keccak

packages.description:
Hybrid typenum-based and const generic array types designed to provide the
flexibility of typenum-based expressions while also allowing interoperability
and a transition path to const generics
packages.name: hybrid-array

packages.description: Testing helpers and future Ethereum conformance fixtures for eth.
packages.name: eth-valkyoth-testkit

packages.description:
Pure Rust implementation of SEC1: Elliptic Curve Cryptography encoding formats including ASN.1 DER
encoded private keys as well as the Elliptic-Curve-Point-to-Octet-String encoding
packages.name: sec1

packages.description: Crunchy unroller: deterministically unroll constant loops
packages.name: crunchy

packages.description:
Compile-time detection heuristics for the optimal word size to use for the
target CPU, which in some cases may differ from its address size a.k.a.
`target_pointer_width`. Implemented as `macro_rules!`
packages.name: cpubits

packages.description:
Constant-time utility library with selection and equality testing support targeting cryptographic
applications. Supports `const fn` where appropriate. Built on the `cmov` crate which provides
architecture-specific predication intrinsics. Heavily inspired by the `subtle` crate.
packages.name: ctutils

packages.description:
Typenum is a Rust library for type-level numbers evaluated at
    compile time. It currently supports bits, unsigned integers, and signed
    integers. It also provides a type-level array of type-level numbers, but its
    implementation is incomplete.
packages.name: typenum

packages.description: Optional sanitization bridge for eth secret-bearing Ethereum data.
packages.name: eth-valkyoth-sanitization

packages.description:
Generic implementation of prime fields built on `crypto-bigint`, along with macros for writing
field element newtypes including ones with formally verified arithmetic using `fiat-crypto`
packages.name: primefield

packages.description: Parser for Rust source code
packages.name: syn

packages.description: no_std Ethereum verification boundaries.
packages.name: eth-valkyoth-verify

packages.description:
Provides extremely fast (uses SIMD on x86_64, aarch64 and wasm32) routines for
1, 2 or 3 byte search and single substring search.
packages.name: memchr

packages.description: Quasi-quoting macro quote!(...)
packages.name: quote

packages.description: Explicit no_std EVM execution boundary for eth.
packages.name: eth-valkyoth-evm

packages.description:
Pure Rust implementation of Base16 a.k.a hexadecimal (RFC 4648) which avoids
any usages of data-dependent branches/LUTs and thereby provides portable
"best effort" constant-time operation and embedded-friendly no_std support
packages.name: base16ct

packages.description:
General purpose Elliptic Curve Cryptography (ECC) support, including traits and generic types for
representing various elliptic curve forms, scalars, points, and public/secret keys composed thereof.
packages.name: elliptic-curve

packages.description: Optional sanitization and RLP derive macros for eth.
packages.name: eth-valkyoth-derive

packages.description: Generic implementation of Hash-based Message Authentication Code (HMAC)
packages.name: hmac
